destinations.evidence: Canonical metadata maps image/audio/video precompile addresses to ImageCall, AudioCall, and VideoCall tags.
destinations.snippet:
AsyncPrecompileMetadata { address: IMAGE_CALL_PRECOMPILE, capability_tag: AsyncPrecompileCapabilityTag::ImageCall, settlement: AsyncPrecompileSettlement::TwoPhase, spc_block_verified: true, short_settlement_enabled: false },
AsyncPrecompileMetadata { address: AUDIO_CALL_PRECOMPILE, capability_tag: AsyncPrecompileCapabilityTag::AudioCall, settlement: AsyncPrecompileSettlement::TwoPhase, spc_block_verified: true, short_settlement_enabled: false },
AsyncPrecompileMetadata { address: VIDEO_CALL_PRECOMPILE, capability_tag: AsyncPrecompileCapabilityTag::VideoCall, settlement: AsyncPrecompileSettlement::TwoPhase, spc_block_verified: true, short_settlement_enabled: false },

destinations.evidence: RPC validation converts the metadata tag to a registry Capability and rejects executors whose capability differs.

equivalence_argument: Because RPC admission uses expected_capability_tag for the exact precompile address, image/audio/video transactions require matching ImageCall/AudioCall/VideoCall executors and reject LLM-only executors.
notes: This supersedes the older generic-LLM mapping for media executor validation.
rationale: The primary merge removes image/audio/video from the LLM capability branch, adds separate required capabilities for each precompile, and extends the TEE registry reader Capability enum with ImageCall=7, AudioCall=8, and VideoCall=9. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: RPC async transaction validation should require IMAGE_CALL precompiles to use executors with ImageCall capability, AUDIO_CALL precompiles to use AudioCall capability, and VIDEO_CALL precompiles to use VideoCall capability, instead of accepting generic LLM capability for those precompiles.
suggested_falsification_tests: Register or mock executors with Llm, ImageCall, AudioCall, and VideoCall capabilities, then validate image/audio/video async transactions and assert each precompile accepts only the matching multimodal capability and rejects an LLM-only executor.
summary: Multimodal precompiles require matching media capabilities.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Register or mock executors with Llm, ImageCall, AudioCall, and VideoCall capabilities, then validate image/audio/video async transactions and assert each precompile accepts only the matching multimodal capability and rejects an LLM-only executor.
title: Multimodal precompiles require matching capabilities
